title: Octane Daemon — Persistent Background Service with IPC, Priority Queue, Cache Policy, and Model Lifecycle
goals: Build the Octane Daemon — persistent background service that transforms Octane from a tool you run to a system that runs for you
Implement pure algorithmic cache policy engine (LRU, TTL tiers, sliding window, pinning, promotion) — no LLM involvement
Implement algorithmic data router for Redis vs Postgres placement using OS-scheduling-inspired rules
Implement OS-level priority queue with anti-starvation aging for daemon task scheduling
Implement daemon shared state management with observable callbacks
Implement aggressive model lifecycle manager with topology-aware idle timeouts
Implement connection pool manager for Redis, Postgres, and Bodega HTTP
Implement Unix socket IPC server with NDJSON protocol for daemon communication
Implement Unix socket IPC client for CLI-side daemon interaction
Implement daemon lifecycle management (start/stop/status/drain) with PID tracking
Wire daemon CLI subcommands into octane main CLI
Write comprehensive true functional tests — no placeholder mocks, real algorithms tested

working_commands: octane daemon start — Fork daemon process, write PID, init pools/server
octane daemon stop — Send SIGTERM to daemon, wait for graceful shutdown
octane daemon status — Show daemon PID, uptime, connection health (Rich table)
octane daemon drain — Gracefully drain queue, reject new items, finish existing

octane/daemon/: cache_policy.py — Pure algorithmic LRU cache engine with TTL tiers, sliding window, pinning, promotion (534 lines)
data_router.py — Algorithmic Redis vs Postgres placement with DataCategory → StorageBackend routing
queue.py — OS-level priority queue (P0-P3) with anti-starvation aging
state.py — Shared daemon state: model registry, connection health, session tracking, observable callbacks
model_manager.py — Aggressive model lifecycle with topology-aware idle timeouts (compact=120s, balanced=300s, power=600s)
pool.py — Connection pool manager for Redis, Postgres, Bodega HTTP with health checks
server.py — Unix socket NDJSON server at ~/.octane/octane.sock with streaming response support
client.py — Unix socket IPC client with daemon auto-detection
lifecycle.py — Daemon process management: fork, PID file, signal handling, stale cleanup
tests/unit/: test_session24_daemon.py — 64 true functional tests across 15 test classes

octane/: main.py — Added daemon_app Typer subgroup with start/stop/status/drain commands
octane/daemon/: cache_policy.py — Fixed PromotionPolicy.should_promote() to accept now parameter for testable age calculation

cache_policy.description: Pure algorithmic cache engine — no I/O, no network. Decides what to keep, evict, and promote.
algorithms: LRU eviction via OrderedDict — O(1) move_to_end/popitem
TTL tiers: EPHEMERAL(5m/15m), SESSION(2h/6h), WARM(24h/72h), PERSISTENT(∞)
Sliding window TTL: each access extends expire_at by base_ttl, capped at created_at + max_ttl
Pinning: critical data immune to LRU eviction
Promotion: data surviving thresholds (access count OR age) graduates Redis → Postgres

data_router.description: Algorithmic placement engine mapping 11 data categories to storage backends.
data_router.categories: QUERY_CACHE, SESSION_STATE, SEARCH_RESULTS, MSR_CONTEXT, RATE_LIMIT, RESEARCH_REPORT, USER_PREFERENCE, MEMORY_CHUNK, MODEL_STATE, MONITOR_CONFIG, CHAIN_CHECKPOINT
data_router.backends: REDIS (ephemeral/session), POSTGRES (persistent/reports), REDIS_WITH_PROMOTION (hot data that may graduate)

queue.description: Priority queue for daemon task scheduling with anti-starvation.
queue.anti_starvation: Items waiting >60s get priority bumped by 1 level

model_manager.description: Aggressive model lifecycle — idle sweep every 30s, topology-aware timeouts.
model_manager.protected_tiers: FAST tier models never unloaded (tiny, always useful)

ipc.streaming: Handlers can yield multiple response dicts for streaming

design_philosophy: OS scheduling theory applied to cache — LRU like page replacement, TTL like process scheduling quanta
Pure algorithmic routing — no LLM calls for data placement decisions
Custom hash functions for Redis key namespacing
Daemon is OPTIONAL — octane ask works without it, daemon just makes it faster
Observable state — callbacks fire on state changes for monitoring

tests.known_failures: 8 matplotlib-dependent chart catalyst tests (pre-existing, no matplotlib installed)
test_classes: TestCachePolicyLRU — 5 tests: real LRU eviction order, capacity enforcement, memory budget
TestCachePolicyTTL — 4 tests: real TTL with time.monotonic, tier-specific lifetimes, expired sweep
TestCachePolicySlidingWindow — 2 tests: sliding window extension, ceiling cap with intermediate accesses
TestCachePolicyPinning — 2 tests: pinned items survive LRU eviction, all-pinned-no-eviction
TestCachePolicyPromotion — 5 tests: access threshold, age threshold, ephemeral never promoted, no re-promotion, stats accuracy
TestDataRouterPolicies — 8 tests: all categories have policies, Redis/Postgres/promotion routing, key hashing, overflow, cache tracking
TestPriorityQueueOrdering — 4 tests: P0 before P3, FIFO within priority, mixed ordering, timeout returns None
TestPriorityQueueAging — 3 tests: P3→P2 aging, P0 never ages, aging reorders queue
TestPriorityQueueDrain — 4 tests: drain rejects new, allows existing, max size, depth by priority
TestDaemonState — 7 tests: model register/unregister, idle detection, callbacks, connection health, sessions, snapshot
TestModelManager — 5 tests: topology thresholds, idle unload, active spared, FAST protected, usage reset
TestUnixSocketIPC — 7 tests: real ping/pong over socket, invalid JSON, multiple requests, connection tracking, daemon detection, connect failure, socket permissions
TestLifecycle — 3 tests: subsystem init, topology resolution, stale socket cleanup
TestPoolManager — 2 tests: pool sizing per topology, snapshot structure
TestCLIIntegration — 2 tests: subcommand registration, status when not running
TestEndToEndDataFlow — 2 tests: full data lifecycle, mixed category routing
tests.test_philosophy: True functional tests — real algorithms with real data structures, real asyncio event loops, real Unix sockets. External dependencies (Bodega HTTP, Redis, Postgres) mocked at the boundary. No placeholder tests.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: PromotionPolicy.should_promote() used entry.age property which calls time.monotonic() live, ignoring the simulated 'now' parameter passed through the call chain
bugs_found_and_fixed.impact: Age-based promotion could never be tested with simulated time; in production, promotion decisions would use wall time instead of consistent monotonic timestamps
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added 'now' parameter to should_promote(), compute age as now - entry.created_at. Updated get_promotion_candidates() to pass now through.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: test_sliding_window_respects_ceiling jumped directly from t+0 to t+800, but EPHEMERAL entries expire at t+300 — entry was dead before the ceiling test could run
bugs_found_and_fixed.impact: Test was asserting on a None entry instead of testing the ceiling cap
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Added intermediate accesses at t+200, t+400, t+600 to keep the entry alive through sliding window extension before testing the ceiling at t+800

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: server.py used AsyncResponseIterator type alias before it was defined (forward reference without quotes)
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Changed Callable signature to use AsyncIterator[dict[str, Any]] directly instead of the forward-referenced alias

key_decisions: Unix socket over TCP — daemon is local-only, no network overhead, natural OS permission model
NDJSON protocol — simple, streamable, debuggable with cat/jq
PID file at ~/.octane/daemon.pid — standard Unix daemon pattern
Anti-starvation aging at 60s — prevents batch tasks from starving under interactive load
FAST tier models protected from unload — 90M model is tiny, always useful for quick completions
Sliding window TTL ceiling = created_at + max_ttl — hot data stays alive but nothing lives forever
Promotion is OR-based (access_count OR age) not AND-based — either signal alone justifies persistence
